steps.title: Find p using the given point
steps.content:
The graph passes through $(-6, 0)$, so $h(-6) = 0$. Substitute $x = -6$ into $h(x) = (x+p)(x-4)(2x-6)$:

$$(-6 + p)(-6 - 4)(2(-6) - 6) = 0$$

$$(-6 + p)(-10)(-18) = 0$$

Since $(-10)(-18) = 180 \neq 0$, the product equals zero only if $-6 + p = 0$, so $p = 6$.

steps.title: Evaluate h(0) with p = 6
steps.content:
Now substitute $x = 0$ and $p = 6$ into the function:

$$h(0) = (0 + 6)(0 - 4)(2(0) - 6)$$

$$h(0) = (6)(-4)(-6)$$

Evaluate: $6 \cdot (-4) = -24$, then $(-24) \cdot (-6) = 144$. That matches choice A — A is correct.
